reforms.description: Iceland broadened its R&D tax credits in 2018, enhancing the incentives for firms to invest in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D investments, the reform is expected to boost innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credits were broadened in 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: The broadening of R&D tax credits represents a significant enhancement of existing innovation support mechanisms, likely leading to increased R&D activities.
reforms.source_quote: Corporate income tax is rather low at 20%, and R&D tax credits were broadened in 2018.

reforms.description: The government is recommended to set up a comprehensive action plan for regulatory reform to reduce the regulatory burden on businesses, which is currently high and hampers investment and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing regulatory burdens can enhance competition and attract investment, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should rapidly implement recommendations to reduce the regulatory burden.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic issues in the regulatory framework that significantly impact business operations and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government should set up a comprehensive action plan for regulatory reform, prioritising reforms that foster competition.

reforms.description: A 5-year national literacy strategy was launched in 2015 to improve reading skills among students, aiming for at least 90% of students to meet minimum reading standards by 2018. This initiative includes the development of reading proficiency standards and new assessment tools.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving literacy skills enhances human capital, which is essential for productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The national literacy strategy was launched and measures are in place to monitor its implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to address literacy challenges, which are crucial for educational outcomes and future workforce capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: A 5-year national literacy strategy was launched in 2015 in the wake of a government White Paper on education reform.

reforms.description: Iceland is reforming its university funding system to prioritize quality and outcomes over quantity, incorporating performance indicators and research criteria into funding allocations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to performance and research outcomes, the reform aims to enhance the quality of education and research, which can lead to greater innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: An ongoing reform of the university funding system aims to shift the focus from quantity to quality and outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how higher education is funded, with potential long-term impacts on the quality of research and education in Iceland.
reforms.source_quote: An ongoing reform of the university funding system aims to shift the focus from quantity to quality and outcomes.

reforms.description: Iceland is considering the introduction of innovation vouchers to encourage collaboration between SMEs and public research institutions for applied R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Innovation vouchers can stimulate R&D activities in SMEs, fostering collaboration with research institutions and enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: Collaborative research can be improved through measures that encourage universities and business to engage more with each other, including innovation vouchers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of innovation vouchers represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration between the private and public sectors, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: These include, for instance, innovation vouchers granted to SMEs to buy industrial or applied R&D from selected public research institutions.

reforms.description: The government plans to establish a sovereign wealth fund to mitigate fiscal effects from economic shocks and diversify risks, primarily funded by dividends from the national power company.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a sovereign wealth fund is expected to stabilize the economy and provide a buffer against economic shocks, which can enhance overall economic resilience and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a sovereign wealth fund represents a significant structural change in fiscal policy aimed at long-term economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to establish a sovereign wealth fund and submitted a draft bill to parliament.

reforms.description: The government is contemplating further mergers of municipalities to improve the effectiveness of public spending and service delivery, particularly in rural areas with small populations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Merging municipalities is expected to enhance efficiency in public service delivery and reduce administrative costs, potentially leading to better resource allocation and improved public services.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated its intention to pursue further municipal mergers.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the initiative focuses on improving local governance rather than a systemic overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently contemplating further mergers.

reforms.description: The government aims to strengthen private funding for universities to enhance ties between research institutions and the business sector, which is currently one of the smallest in the OECD.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing private funding for universities is expected to foster collaboration between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has expressed its intention to pursue this initiative publicly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to improve the funding landscape for universities, although it does not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: More funding from the private sector, including from abroad, could further foster tertiary education and strengthen ties between research institutions and the business sector.
